analyses.hook: Intro trip hunting in 2026 is brutal. Especially as an engineering student.
analyses.cta: Click the link in my bio and check it out today.
evidence_limitations: No visual evidence of the platform's interface or listings.
No visual evidence of the speaker's identity or credentials.
Visual evidence is limited to text overlays and basic visual cues.

analyses.hook: I just found out I've been appointed fake jobs this entire time.
analyses.cta: Stop wasting your time on applications for jobs that don't exist.
evidence_limitations: No visual evidence of the actual JobWrite AI's Intern List website interface
No visual evidence of the specific job sectors mentioned

analyses.hook: 3 reasons interns don't get return offers (that have nothing to do with the work)
evidence_limitations: Limited visual context due to single static shot
No audio transcript provided for precise speech analysis
Visual evidence is limited to described scenes

analyses.hook: Slavery did not end. It was rebranded into internships.
evidence_limitations: No visual evidence of specific companies or internships is provided.
The claim about internships being a rebranded form of slavery is not visually supported.
Limited visual context beyond the described scenes
No additional audio cues beyond the woman's frustration

analyses.hook: Three things I did during my freshman year of mechanical engineering that got me a Fortune 500 internship.
analyses.cta: Be persistent and show that you are actively seeking new opportunities.
evidence_limitations: No visual evidence of the speaker's personal projects or research opportunities.
No visual evidence of the speaker's networking activities.

evidence_boundaries: Apify first collects a broad public surface; Codex then allocates the separate TwelveLabs budget to a smaller documented subset.
The 5 USD Apify cap includes both broad discovery and selected-item media retrieval.
Codex selection is constrained by novelty, platform coverage, account concentration, and explicit evidence rationales.
Failed publication attempts remain in cumulative provider accounting; a recovery run never resets the budget.
The scheduled job reads only the approved public URLs in the committed intake manifest.
Costs are provider-reported actuals when available and conservative estimates otherwise.
Partial means every requested URL reconciled and at least 80% received TwelveLabs analysis; it does not mean complete analysis coverage.
Creative descriptions are provider observations, not causal performance claims.
Credentials, downloaded media, raw provider payloads, and the semantic database are not published.
errors: twelvelabs:visible_proof[9] must have a valid timestamp range within the video duration.
twelvelabs:TwelveLabs asset create:media_url_not_accessible:Cannot access the media URL '[provider media URL]

orchestration.selection_strategy: Recover the failed media-coverage gate without widening provider budgets: retain the six TikTok and Instagram candidates that already proved retrievable, replace the unavailable Instagram asset and the three metadata-only YouTube rows with four current, strategically distinct TikTok and Instagram videos, and preserve two-platform coverage with unique accounts.

selections.why_now: This retrievable breakout candidate frames fake and ghost jobs as a concrete risk and contrasts third-party listings with direct company applications.
The thirty-minutes-a-day interview-preparation promise is useful for examining pacing, visible proof, and how the call to action supports the claim.
An engineering student narrates tab-switching fatigue before presenting a consolidated internship feed, adding a current problem-solution and sponsored-claim case.
The tell-me-about-yourself elimination framing offers a structured interview-answer contrast and a clear opportunity to inspect visible story-building guidance.
This high-engagement argument about unpaid internships and replacement labor broadens the corpus beyond tactics into compensation and access constraints.
A practical alternative for students without a summer internship can show how a creator turns anxiety into specific project actions.
The freshman-to-Fortune-500 narrative supplies a concrete experience-building sequence to compare with tool-led and fear-led hooks.
Return-offer behavior is underrepresented, and this candidate promises three non-work reasons that may expose a distinct list-and-warning structure.
A current day-in-the-life college-intern video adds an experiential format that can reveal pacing and proof patterns missing from advice-heavy candidates.
The concise hundred-applications-without-results setup captures application-volume frustration and supplies a high-emotion contrast to prescriptive job-search content.
